articles.title: Dow Soars 875 Points to Record Close; S&P 500 Overcomes Broadcom-Led Tech Pullback
articles.description: Dow surged +1.73% to record 51,561.93; S&P 500 +0.41% to 7,584.31; Nasdaq -0.09% to 26,830.96. Massive rotation from tech/AI chips into healthcare, financials, and small-caps. Broadcom -13% after unchanged AI outlook. UnitedHealth +5% on BofA upgrade. Russell 2000 +1.4% outperformed S&P 500.

articles.title: Broadcom Set to Shed $300 Billion in Value as AI Results Fail to Impress
articles.description: Broadcom -14% after Q2 revenue $22.19B missed $22.27B estimate. AI chip forecast reiterated at $100B for FY2027 — unchanged from March, disappointing investors. Stock had rallied 38% YTD into earnings. P/E 29.9x vs. S&P 500 27.94x. CEO Hock Tan announced shift to 'chips only' strategy for AI, moving away from full integrated systems.

articles.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Q2 2026 Earnings: Revenue Miss, AI Forecast Unchanged
articles.description: Revenue $22.19B vs $22.27B est (LSEG). Adj EPS $2.44 vs $2.40 est — slight beat. Infrastructure software $7.18B vs $7.32B est. AI revenue $10.8B, more than doubled YoY. Q3 guidance $29.4B, beating $28.53B est. Full-year AI chip sales unchanged at 'in excess of $100B'. Stock plunged ~15%.

articles.title: CrowdStrike Sinks 9% as Earnings Beat Falls Short of Lofty Expectations
articles.description: CRWD -8-10% after Q1 earnings beat but guidance in-line. Announced 4-for-1 stock split effective next month. Revenue ~$1.44B guidance vs $1.3B est. CEO Kurtz: 'CrowdStrike is AI security infrastructure.' Stock had rallied 60%+ YTD into earnings, setting high bar.

articles.title: Schwab Market Update: Broadcom Plunge Takes Chip Out of Long Rally
articles.description: Vix 16.38, +2%. S&P 500 tech up 17% in past month but Magnificent Seven fraying. Oil -3.3% to $92.83 after Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Bitcoin $63,985, -2.4% — 13 straight days of ETF outflows, >$3B. Crypto Fear & Greed in extreme fear. Bitcoin support near $60K. Weekly jobless claims 225K, 3-month high. Private credit fears: Blackstone capped BCRED withdrawals.

articles.title: UnitedHealth Stock Climbs After Major BofA Upgrade to Buy, PT $450
articles.description: UNH +5% after Bank of America upgrade from Neutral to Buy. PT raised to $450 from $420. Analyst Kevin Fischbeck: improving medical cost trends, lower utilization. Data points 'make it harder to believe Q1 strength was only temporary.' Q2 earnings expected next month.

articles.title: Blackstone Private Credit Fund Caps Withdrawals as Redemption Requests Jump to 10%
articles.description: Blackstone's $79B BCRED capped withdrawals at 5% after Q2 redemption requests hit 10% of shares (up from 7.9% in Q1). Fund shrank from $82B. Class I shares 9.3% annualized return since inception, 50% premium to leveraged loans. BX shares rose 8% — '10% is better than feared' per Evercore. Industry-wide retreat from private credit by wealthy individuals.

articles.title: SpaceX Targets $135 IPO Price at $1.77 Trillion Valuation
articles.description: SpaceX sets fixed $135/share IPO price (unusual vs. range). 555.6M shares + 83.3M greenshoe. $75B raise (+$11.2B greenshoe). $1.77 trillion valuation — 7th largest U.S. company. Musk retains >82% voting control. Nasdaq: SPCX. Goldman Sachs lead underwriter. Expected debut June 12. Largest IPO ever, 3x Alibaba record. Tesla holds $2.56B in SpaceX shares.

articles.title: Alphabet to Raise $80 Billion from Stock Sales to Fund AI Build-out
articles.description: Alphabet plans $80B equity raise: $10B Berkshire private placement, $30B underwritten offerings, $40B ATM program. Capex guidance $180-190B in 2026. Hyperscalers (MSFT, AMZN, META, GOOGL) on track for $700B+ combined capex. BCA Research: 'Equity funding suggests AI capex cycle entering mature, capital-intensive phase.'

articles.title: Ciena Reports Q2 2026: Revenue +40% YoY to $1.57B, Adj EPS $1.64 (+290%)
articles.description: Ciena Q2 revenue $1.57B (+40% YoY), adj EPS $1.64 (+290% YoY from $0.42). Adj gross margin 44.9% vs 41.0%. Operating margin 19.5% vs 8.2%. Raised FY guidance: revenue $6.3B ±$100M, op margin ~19%. CEO: 'Structural, multi-year opportunities from AI-driven demand.' Stock fell 8% despite beat-and-raise — similar to Broadcom dynamic.

articles.title: Lululemon Q1 FY2026: EPS $1.69, Revenue $2.47B (+4%), Operating Margin Collapses to 11.2%
articles.description: LULU Q1 EPS $1.69 vs $2.60 (-35%), revenue $2.47B (+4%). Operating margin 11.2% vs 18.5% (-730bps). Americas -3% constant dollar. International +16%. FY guidance cut: revenue $11.0-11.15B (flat YoY). EPS $10.95-11.15. Stock down 40% YTD. Interim co-CEOs cite 'headwinds' and 'additional actions to reposition.'

articles.title: Quantinuum Opens at $68/Share on Nasdaq, +13.3% Above $60 IPO Price
articles.description: Quantinuum (QNT) opened at $68/share on Nasdaq (+13.3% vs $60 IPO). Raised $1.68B. Market cap ~$17.6B at first trade. Full-stack quantum computing company merged from Honeywell Quantum + Cambridge Quantum. Priced above $53-55 range. Commerce Dept providing up to $100M.

articles.title: Dell Skyrockets 32% After Blowout AI Server Earnings — Best Day Ever
articles.description: DELL +32.76% after earnings (May 29). AI server revenue +757% YoY to $16.1B. Adj EPS $4.86 vs $2.94 est. Revenue +88% YoY. Stock +234% YTD. CEO Michael Dell net worth $233B, surpassing Zuckerberg and Huang. Morgan Stanley: 'one of the most impressive quarters we've seen.' Dell awarded $9.7B Pentagon software contract.

articles.title: ADP: Private Payrolls Surge 122K in May, Beating 110K Estimate
articles.description: ADP May +122K (strongest since Jan 2025), beat 110K consensus. Broad-based gains across 8 of 10 sectors. Education/health +57K. Annual wage growth: job-stayers 4.4%, switchers 6.5%. Friday BLS report: consensus +80K payrolls, unemployment steady 4.3%. Fed meeting June 16-17: market pricing hold at 3.5-3.75%.

articles.title: Oil Retreats 2.8% on Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire; Gold +0.9% Above $4,500
articles.description: WTI crude -2.8% to $93.30/bbl; Brent -2.5% to $95.35. Israel-Lebanon ceasefire renewal + Trump reportedly ruling out all-out war with Iran unless US troops killed. Gold +0.9% to ~$4,508/oz. DXY -0.1% to 99.42. 10Y yield ~4.48%. Still: ~15M barrels/day supply lost; 170 ships locked in Persian Gulf. WSJ: up to 6 months to return to 80% pre-crisis supply.

articles.title: Bitcoin ~$63,500: 13 Straight Days of ETF Outflows, Fear & Greed in Extreme Fear
articles.description: Bitcoin -3.2% to $63,478.80 at 5:25 PM EDT. Worst week since February. 13 straight days of net outflows from spot ETFs (>$3B). Down ~30% YTD. Crypto Fear & Greed Index in extreme fear. Support expected near $60,000 (efficient miner cost + 200-week MA). MSTR, COIN down 1%+. AI stock euphoria may have drawn capital away from crypto.

articles.title: Strategy (MSTR) Drops 31% in a Month After First Bitcoin Sale in Years
articles.description: MSTR -31% in a month after Strategy broke no-sell rule, offloading 32 Bitcoin for $2.5M to cover preferred dividends. Stock trading near $120-138 range. COIN hovering near $179.50. MSTR forward P/E -3.15. Market cap $45.7B.

articles.title: CNBC Market Close: Rotation Away from Tech — 'Early Innings of a Rotation'
articles.description: Dow +874.86 (+1.73%) to 51,561.93 record. S&P +0.41% to 7,584.31. Nasdaq -0.09%. Broadcom worst day since Jan 2025. CrowdStrike worst day since July 2024. Russell 2000 +1.4% outperformed. Dennis Follmer (CIO Montis Financial): 'This rally is getting tired after an incredible more than two-month surge... early innings of a rotation.' Initial claims 225K vs 215K est (3-month high).

articles.title: Apple WWDC Next Week: New AI Features, Enhanced Siri; BofA PT $380
articles.description: Apple +14% YTD ahead of WWDC (Monday June 8). BofA raised PT to $380 (from $330). New AI features expected: enhanced Siri, possible standalone chatbot. Thesis: device/platform owner could become more valuable than AI model provider if Siri becomes dominant interface.

articles.title: Beige Book: Widespread Fuel-Driven Inflation Complaints; Fed Concerns on Iran War Impact
articles.description: Fed Beige Book: widespread complaints about fuel-driven inflation. Higher transportation costs passing through to other products. Fed officials increasingly concerned about Iran war's impact on prices; may need to raise rates again. Productivity revised down to +0.3% from +0.8%. Unit labor costs +1.8%. Friday jobs report: consensus +80-85K payrolls.
